channel.title: Cybersecurity News Aggregator
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity news from top sources
channel.comment: News aggregated from: Krebs on Security, The Hacker News, Threatpost, Bleeping Computer, Dark Reading

item.title: Critical NGINX Vulnerability Can Crash Workers and May Allow Remote Code Execution
item.description: F5 has shipped fixes for a critical nginx flaw that lets a remote, unauthenticated attacker trigger a heap buffer overflow in the worker process with crafted HTTP requests. CVE-2026-42533 was patched ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Hackers abuse ViPNet software to target Russian govt agencies
item.description: An advanced threat actor is abusing the update mechanism for the ViPNet private networking product suite to target Russian organizations, including government agencies. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: UAC-0145 Uses ClickFix CAPTCHAs to Infect Ukrainian Devices wih Malware
item.description:
Russian state-sponsored threat actors have been observed leveraging the infamous ClickFix strategy to trick Ukrainian targets into infecting their own machines with data-stealing malware.
According t...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: SonicWall SMA Zero-Days Exploited Before Disclosure to Gain Root Access
item.description: A previously undocumented threat actor has been attributed to the exploitation of recently disclosed SonicWall Secure Mobile Access (SMA) 1000 series VPN appliances as zero-days prior their public dis...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Update now: 7-Zip fixes RCE flaw exploitable with malicious archives
item.description: 7-Zip version 26.02 was released on June 25 to fix a remote code execution vulnerability that could allow attackers to execute malicious code by convincing users to open specially crafted compressed f...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: WordPress Core "wp2shell" RCE flaws get public exploits, patch now
item.description: Public exploits have been released for the critical "wp2shell" remote code execution vulnerabilities affecting WordPress Core, making it imperative that administrators patch their sites immediately. [...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Microsoft warns of surge in ACR Stealer attacks on customers
item.description: Microsoft has observed a surge in attacks using the ACR Stealer malware to steal browser-stored passwords, authentication tokens, and sensitive documents from its enterprise customers. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: The Future of Age Verification: Your Face Never Leaves Your Device
item.description: As age verification laws expand worldwide, organizations face growing pressure to protect users' privacy while meeting regulatory requirements. Incode explains how on-device age estimation verifies ag...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: New wp2shell WordPress Core Flaw Lets Unauthenticated Attackers Run Code
item.description: Updated July 18, 2026: the two flaws now carry CVE IDs, the full mechanism has been published, a persistent-object-cache condition has surfaced, and a working proof-of-concept is public. The story bel...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Abbott probes two cyber incidents amid extortion claims
item.description: Abbott Laboratories is investigating two separate cybersecurity incidents after confirming unauthorized access to internal legacy Exact Sciences systems in its Cancer Diagnostics business, while also ...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: OpenSSL HollowByte Flaw Could Freeze Server Memory with 11-Byte TLS Requests
item.description: Eleven bytes will make an unpatched OpenSSL server set aside up to 131 KB of memory for a message that never arrives. On the glibc systems Okta tested, that memory is gone until the process restarts. ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Inc Ransomware Exploits SonicWall SMA Zero-Days
item.description: When chained together, the two vulnerabilities allow threat actors to gain root-level capabilities on SonicWall's mobile access appliances....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Seven Malicious Vite npm Packages Use Blockchain C2 to Deliver a RAT
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a cluster of seven malicious npm packages targeting the Vite frontend tooling ecosystem as part of a software supply chain attack.
The malicious package camp...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: HollowByte DDoS flaw bloats OpenSSL server memory with 11-byte payload
item.description: A vulnerability dubbed HollowByte allows unauthenticated attackers to trigger a denial-of-service (DoS) condition on OpenSSL servers with a malicious payload of just 11 bytes. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: New NadMesh Botnet Hunts Exposed AI Services for Cloud Keys and Kubernetes Tokens
item.description:
A Go botnet called NadMesh turned up in early July hunting exposed AI services, and the operator's own dashboard claims 3,811 unique AWS keys.
A Shodan harvester keeps the scan queue stocked with Com...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: The Real AI Threat Is Blind Trust
item.description: AI models left to both interpret and execute commands eliminate critical cybersecurity oversight....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: GoldenEyeDog Subgroup Linked to DigiCert Breach and Code-Signing Certificate Theft
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have attributed the April 2026 DigiCert security incident to a threat activity cluster dubbed CylindricalCanine.
Expel, which shared technical details of the event, describe...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Ernst & Young discloses data breach after support system hack
item.description: Ernst & Young is notifying customers of a data breach caused by the compromise of a third-party support ticket system used by its IT personnel. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Inside the Search for "Clean" Residential Proxies for Carding
item.description: Residential proxies are no longer the silver bullet they once were for carding. Flare explains why cybercriminals increasingly seek "clean" residential proxies and combine them with browser fingerprin...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Fake Coding Tests Deliver OtterCookie-Aligned Malware Hidden in SVG Flag Images
item.description: North Korean threat actors linked to the Contagious Interview campaign have been observed employing steganography in SVG image files to conceal malicious payloads as part of a campaign using fake job ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Gold Eagle Clearinghouse Targets Security Gap, but How Is Unclear
item.description: The White House launched Gold Eagle to coordinate vulnerability response in a new AI world, but multiple questions linger over how it's being implemented....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Google Bets 'Agentic Defense' Strategy Can Outpace Attackers
item.description: Google Cloud incorporates key Wiz capabilities into an agentic defense platform to automate threat detection and remediation against AI attacks....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: E.U. Orders Google to Open Android Mic, Camera and Screen to Rival AI Assistants
item.description: The European Commission on Thursday ordered Google to give rival AI assistants the same reach into Android that Gemini already has: the camera, the microphone, whatever is on screen, a wake word that ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: The Race to Field Military Autonomy Is On, Can Trusted Information Infrastructure Keep Pace?
item.description: Military forces are under increasing pressure to field autonomous capabilities faster than ever before. Across the U.S., UK, and NATO, new investment, evolving defense strategies, and accelerated acqu...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: New Windows LegacyHive zero-day gives hackers admin privileges
item.description: A security researcher using the "Nightmare Eclipse" handle has released a Windows zero-day exploit dubbed LegacyHive that allows attackers to escalate privileges on up-to-date Windows systems. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Armenia Detains Russian Tourist on U.S. Warrant for REvil Hacker, Lawyers Say Wrong Man
item.description:
Armenia has held a Russian tourist named Aleksandr Ermakov in a detention center since June 28, on a U.S. extradition request for a REvil ransomware suspect named Aleksandr Ermakov.
His wife, Maria Y...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Windows Server 2022 reach end of mainstream support in 90 days
item.description: Microsoft announced that Windows Server 2022 will reach the mainstream end date in October 2026, but will switch to extended support and continue receiving security updates for five more years. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: ACR Stealer Uses ClickFix Lures to Steal Browser Tokens and Microsoft 365 Files
item.description: ACR Stealer, an infostealer in circulation since 2024, is walking out of enterprise networks with saved browser passwords, live session tokens, PDFs, Microsoft 365 documents, and files from synced One...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: New GoSerpent Malware Targets Southeast Asian Governments and Diplomats for Espionage
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a previously undocumented malware called GoSerpent that has been put to use in cyber attacks targeting entities in Southeast Asia since late 2025 with a focus...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: US charges two over laundering $43 million from investment fraud
item.description: U.S. prosecutors on Thursday charged a New York man and woman for their roles in a large-scale crime ring that laundered money stolen in cyber investment fraud scams. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer
